# PaperCache

## Overview
PaperCache is a lightning-fast, computational scratchpad built for developers who need their notes to actually do work. Inspired by AntiNote, but 100% free and open-source.

## Key Features
- **Global Hotkey Summon**: Summon it with a hotkey anywhere, jot down thoughts, and dismiss.
- **Reactive Math**: Built-in support for evaluating mathematical expressions directly inside the notes.
- **Inline AI**: AI capabilities embedded into the markdown experience for context-aware assistance.
- **Tasks**: Manage tasks effectively within a plain markdown environment.
- **Zero-Friction**: Spawn exactly where your cursor is, hide markdown syntax for clean reading, and maintain a true zero-idle CPU state to preserve battery life.

## Architecture
PaperCache was originally built using Electron, but has recently been migrated to Rust and Tauri. This shift was critical for significantly reducing memory footprint, improving cold start times, and creating a native feel across macOS, Windows, and Linux. The performance audit of this migration can be found at: https://github.com/VariableThe/PaperCache/blob/main/PERFORMANCE_AUDIT.md

## Codebase
The primary repository is hosted at: https://github.com/VariableThe/PaperCache
It features a web-based UI built with React and Vite, powered by a Tauri/Rust backend.
